titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5944

p.seg: To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear the present writing, Ralph Noble sends greeting in the Lord.

by having sealed the present charter with my seal, to have obliged myself and my heirs for ever, that on account of the charter of the Prior and Convent of the Church of Durham which they made for me concerning the land which I shall hold of them by right of heritage in the toun of Lamberton, to wit, one toft, and four acres of land, as it is specified in their charter composed concerning that matter, I shall not seek defence or warrandice from the said Prior and Convent if it should happen that a demand be moved by anyone in a clerical or a lay court over the said land, and that I shall keep the Prior and Convent of Durham, and the Prior and Convent of Coldingham free from financial loss in all things as to the aforesaid land. And if a plea should be moved upon it, I and my heirs shall support the expenses of the whole of the plea; and that we shall keep inviolate faith on this side.
In testimony of this matter, I have put my seal to the present writing for myself and my heirs.

With these witnesses: the lord William of Mordington; the lord William of Scremerston; Adam of Prenderguest; Robert son of Gregory of Coldingham; David of Lumsdaine; Adam son of John of Over Ayton; John son of Ingram, of the same toun; Bertram of Reston; Patrick Scott; Gregory of Beoch.
